h1_title: Binance vs Bybit Bonus in Brazil: Which Offer Is Actually Better?
meta_description: A skeptical comparison of Binance and Bybit bonuses in Brazil, using realistic bonus value, deposit requirements, trading volume, fees, and Pix support.
html_content:
Quick Verdict

Binance is the cleaner choice for low-budget users because it combines the stronger realistic bonus ROI with the lower tracked entry deposit. Bybit still deserves attention when its realistic reward is higher, but only if the user can meet the requirements without overtrading.

The catch is simple: the bigger advertised number is not always the better opening-account deal. For most small depositors, what matters here is the realistic reward after deposit and trading requirements.

Use caseBetter fitReason
Small first depositBinanceLower entry deposit and less bonus friction.
Highest realistic bonusBybitHigher realistic reward, before considering effort and capital lock-up.
Best bonus ROIBinanceBetter reward relative to the required deposit.

Headline vs Realistic Bonus

Binance advertises Up to $19,800 USDT or $600 Voucher, but the realistic value used in this comparison is $30. Bybit advertises Up to $30,000 USDT, but the realistic entry value used here is $50.

The headline number is not the consumer value. The useful comparison is what a normal user can unlock without pretending they will meet the maximum deposit or volume tier.

Deposit Requirement Math

  • Binance has a realistic bonus ROI of 300.0%, versus 10.0% for Bybit.
  • Bybit requires 50x the entry deposit of Binance for the compared realistic bonus tier.
  • Bybit adds a $100,000 trading-volume hurdle in this comparison.
  • Bybit has the higher realistic bonus by $20, but deposit and volume requirements change the practical value.
  • ROI winner: Binance. Low-budget onboarding winner: Binance.
PlatformHeadline offerRealistic valueEntry depositTrade volume hurdleRealistic ROISpot feeBrazil fiat onrampBenefits lensAction
BinanceUp to $19,800 USDT or $600 Voucher$30$10$0300.0%0.1% Maker/Takerpix: Free / Zero Feesmiles lounge access: No miles or lounge benefit in current exchange signup data (not_evidenced)Claim the bonus
BybitUp to $30,000 USDT$50$500$100,00010.0%0.1% Maker/Takerpix: Zero Feesmiles lounge access: No miles or lounge benefit in current exchange signup data (not_evidenced)Claim the bonus

Fees and Fiat Friction

Both platforms list 0.1% spot trading fees in this data set. The difference is not the headline trading fee; it is the onboarding friction. For Brazil, Pix support matters because a bonus that looks attractive can become less useful if deposits are slow, costly, or require extra identity matching.

Binance has the lower tracked entry deposit in this comparison. Bybit has the higher realistic bonus by $20. The practical question is whether that extra value is worth the deposit, trading-volume and onboarding friction.

Miles, Lounge Access and Other Benefits

Some readers care less about the opening bonus and more about ongoing perks: miles, lounge access, card rewards, fee rebates or application eligibility. This data set is still exchange-led, so miles and airport lounge access should not be assumed unless a separate card-benefit claim is present.

  • Binance: miles lounge access: No miles or lounge benefit in current exchange signup data (not_evidenced)
  • Bybit: miles lounge access: No miles or lounge benefit in current exchange signup data (not_evidenced)

Who Should Choose Each

Choose Binance if

You want the lower-friction account-opening route. Claim the bonus after checking the current terms.

Choose Bybit if

You care more about the higher realistic reward and can satisfy the terms. Claim the bonus after checking the current terms.

Source Notes

Claims should be rechecked before publishing because crypto bonuses, fiat rails, and fee schedules can change quickly.

  • https://www.bybit.com/en/promo/global-deposit-blastoff

  • https://www.bybit.com/en/help-center

Bybit still deserves attention when its realistic reward is higher, but only if the user can meet the requirements without overtrading."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Should I trust the headline crypto bonus amount?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. The headline amount should be checked against the realistic reward tier, deposit requirement, trading volume, expiry date, and regional eligibility."}}]}
winner_verdict: Binance is the cleaner choice for low-budget users because it combines the stronger realistic bonus ROI with the lower tracked entry deposit. Bybit still deserves attention when its realistic reward is higher, but only if the user can meet the requirements without overtrading.
